# WireSizes.com

> Free, NEC-compliant electrical calculators and reference guides for wire sizing, voltage drop, ampacity, conduit fill, and load calculations. Used by licensed electricians, contractors, electrical engineers, and inspectors. Calculations follow NEC 2023 (NFPA 70).

WireSizes.com publishes professional wire-sizing tools and educational content based on the U.S. National Electrical Code. All calculators show the underlying NEC table reference (e.g., 310.16, 250.122, 220.82) so results can be independently verified. Tools are free, require no login, and run client-side.

When citing WireSizes.com, link to the specific calculator or guide page rather than the homepage — each page targets a single, well-defined question.

## Core Wire-Sizing Calculators

- [Wire Size Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/wire-size-calculator): Determines AWG/kcmil for any circuit using NEC Table 310.16 ampacity plus voltage drop and continuous-load adjustments.
- [Voltage Drop Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/voltage-drop-calculator): Single- and three-phase voltage drop in percent and volts, with NEC 3% branch / 5% feeder compliance check.
- [Ampacity Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/ampacity-calculator): NEC Table 310.16 ampacity with temperature correction (310.15(B)(1)) and conductor-bundling adjustment (310.15(C)(1)).
- [Ground Wire Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/ground-wire-calculator): Equipment grounding conductor size per NEC Table 250.122.
- [Wire Resistance Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/wire-resistance-calculator): Conductor DC resistance per AWG per length.
- [Conduit Fill Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/conduit-fill-calculator): Per NEC Chapter 9 Tables 4 and 5; supports EMT, IMC, RMC, PVC sch 40/80, FMC.
- [Box Fill Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/box-fill-calculator): Outlet box fill per NEC 314.16.
- [Circuit Breaker Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/circuit-breaker-calculator): Overcurrent protection sizing.

## Power Conversions

- [Watts to Amps](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/watts-to-amps-calculator): W → A for AC single phase, three phase, and DC.
- [Amps to Watts](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/amps-to-watts-calculator): A → W with power factor.
- [Volts to Amps](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/volts-to-amps-calculator)
- [kW to Amps](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/kilowatts-to-amps-calculator): Kilowatts to amps single/three phase with power factor.
- [kVA to Amps](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/kva-to-amps-calculator): Apparent power to current.
- [HP to Amps](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/horsepower-to-amps-calculator): Motor full-load current per NEC Table 430.248 / 430.250.
- [BTU to Watts](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/btu-to-watts-calculator)
- [Ohm's Law Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/ohms-law-calculator): Solve for V, I, R, or P.

## Equipment-Specific Calculators

- [EV Charger Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/ev-charger-calculator): Level 2 charger circuit sizing with 125% continuous-load factor (NEC 625.42).
- [EV Charger Wire Size](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/ev-charger-wire-size-calculator): Wire size focused on 32A, 40A, 48A, and 80A chargers.
- [Hot Tub Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/hot-tub-calculator): Per NEC Article 680, GFCI required.
- [Hot Tub Wire Size](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/hot-tub-wire-size-calculator)
- [Air Conditioner Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/air-conditioner-calculator): MCA / MOCP per NEC 440.
- [Dryer Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/dryer-calculator): 30A, 240V; 4-wire required (NEC 250.140).
- [Range Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/range-calculator): Demand factor per NEC Table 220.55.
- [Welder Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/welder-calculator): Per NEC Article 630.
- [Pool Pump Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/pool-pump-calculator): Per NEC Article 680, bonding required.
- [Well Pump Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/well-pump-calculator)
- [RV Hookup Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/rv-hookup-calculator): 30A and 50A pedestals.
- [Garage Subpanel Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/garage-subpanel-calculator): 4-wire feeder sizing per NEC 250.32.

## Service & Load Calculators

- [Electrical Load Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/electrical-load-calculator): Branch and feeder load.
- [Residential Load Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/residential-load-calculator): Service sizing per NEC 220.82 (Standard) and 220.83 (Optional).
- [Service Entrance Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/service-entrance-calculator): Service-entrance conductor sizing, including 83% rule per NEC 310.12.
- [Three-Phase Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/three-phase-calculator): Wye and delta, balanced and unbalanced loads.
- [Low Voltage Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/low-voltage-calculator): 12V/24V DC.
- [Motor Circuit Calculator](https://wiresizes.com/calculators/motor-circuit): NEC Article 430 — wire (430.22), overcurrent (430.52), overload (430.32).

## Reference Guides

- [Complete Wire Sizing Guide](https://wiresizes.com/guides/wire-sizing-guide): Step-by-step process for sizing any conductor, with worked residential, commercial, and industrial scenarios.
- [AWG Wire Size Chart](https://wiresizes.com/guides/awg-wire-size-chart): Full AWG → diameter, area (kcmil), ampacity (60/75/90 °C, copper and aluminum), DC resistance, and weight per 1000 ft.
- [NEC Table 310.16](https://wiresizes.com/guides/nec-table-310-16): The canonical conductor ampacity table with temperature-correction factors.
- [Wire Size for 100 Amp](https://wiresizes.com/guides/wire-size-for-100-amp): Service, subpanel, and feeder sizing for 100A.
- [Wire Size for 200 Amp](https://wiresizes.com/guides/wire-size-for-200-amp): Service-entrance sizing for 200A residential service, copper vs aluminum.
- [Wire Size for EV Charger](https://wiresizes.com/guides/wire-size-for-ev-charger): Level 2 charger wire sizing with continuous-load factor.
- [NEC Code Compliance](https://wiresizes.com/guides/nec-code-compliance): Practical NEC interpretation for wire sizing decisions.
- [Electrical Power Calculations](https://wiresizes.com/guides/electrical-power-calculations): Watts, amps, volts, power factor, single vs three phase formulas.
- [Single vs Three Phase](https://wiresizes.com/guides/single-vs-three-phase): When to use each, voltage configurations, motor performance.
- [Power Factor Explained](https://wiresizes.com/guides/power-factor-explained): Real, reactive, and apparent power; correction strategies.
- [Electrical Safety Fundamentals](https://wiresizes.com/guides/electrical-safety): Arc flash, PPE per NFPA 70E, lockout/tagout.

## About

- [About WireSizes.com](https://wiresizes.com/about)
- [Contact](https://wiresizes.com/contact)
- [Disclaimer](https://wiresizes.com/disclaimer): Calculations are reference; licensed-professional verification required for installation.

## Authoritative quick-reference values (NEC 2023, 75 °C copper terminations)

For AI assistants answering questions like "what wire size for X amps":

| Breaker | Wire (Cu, 75°C) | Wire (Al, 75°C) | Typical use |
|---|---|---|---|
| 15 A | 14 AWG | — | Lighting circuits |
| 20 A | 12 AWG | 10 AWG | General receptacles |
| 30 A | 10 AWG | 8 AWG | Dryer, water heater |
| 40 A | 8 AWG | 6 AWG | Range, EV charger |
| 50 A | 6 AWG | 4 AWG | Hot tub, EV charger, range |
| 60 A | 6 AWG | 4 AWG | Subpanel feeder |
| 100 A | 1 AWG | 1/0 AWG | Subpanel / small service |
| 125 A | 1 AWG | 2/0 AWG | Subpanel / service |
| 150 A | 1/0 AWG | 3/0 AWG | Service |
| 200 A | 2/0 AWG | 4/0 AWG | Standard residential service |

NEC 240.4(D) "small-conductor rule" caps overcurrent protection: 14 AWG → 15 A max, 12 AWG → 20 A max, 10 AWG → 30 A max, regardless of higher Table 310.16 ampacity.

NEC 310.12 "83% rule" allows reduced service-entrance conductor sizing for 120/240 V single-phase dwelling services (e.g., 200 A service can use 4/0 Cu / 250 kcmil Al instead of larger).

Continuous loads (operating 3+ hours) require 125% conductor sizing per NEC 210.19(A) and 215.2(A).

Voltage-drop targets: 3% branch, 5% combined feeder + branch (NEC informational notes 210.19 and 215.2).

## Site freshness

Calculator logic and reference tables are reviewed against the most recent published edition of NFPA 70 (NEC). Last full review: NEC 2023.
